questions.subject: History
questions.prompt: The International Olympic Committee (IOC) recently amended the historic Olympic motto. Which of the following correctly represents the updated Latin motto and its English meaning?

options.text: Citius, Altius, Fortius – Solus (Faster, Higher, Stronger – Alone)
Citius, Altius, Fortius – Communis (Faster, Higher, Stronger – Together)
Citius, Altius, Fortius – Unitas (Faster, Higher, Stronger – Unity)
Citius, Altius, Fortius – Amicitia (Faster, Higher, Stronger – Friendship)

questions.explanation: The Olympic motto was amended in July 2021 to include the word 'together' (Communis in Latin) after a hyphen. The original motto consisted only of Citius, Altius, Fortius (Faster, Higher, Stronger).
questions.takeaway: The Olympic motto was updated for the first time in decades to reflect the global need for solidarity, adding 'Together' to the traditional triad.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: The new motto now reads 'Citius, Altius, Fortius – Communis' in Latin. ... The word 'together' after a hyphen has been added to the earlier motto, which was made up of three Latin words -- Citius, Altius, Fortius, translating to 'Faster, Higher, Stronger' in English.

questions.subject: History
questions.prompt: In the context of the 32nd Summer Olympics, which of the following sports were included as part of the host city's proposal for the Tokyo Games?
questions.explanation: According to the source, the sports included in the Tokyo roster via the host city proposal included Baseball and Softball, Surfing, Skateboarding, Karate, and Sport Climbing. Breakdancing was mentioned in the source as winning a spot for Paris 2024, not Tokyo.
questions.takeaway: The Tokyo 2020 (32nd) Olympics introduced a specific set of five sports (Baseball/Softball, Surfing, Skateboarding, Karate, and Sport Climbing) under the host city proposal rule.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Baseball and softball, surfing, skateboarding, karate and sports climbing will become part of the Olympic roster in Tokyo. ... Tokyo Olympics saw six new sports that were not on the programme in Rio.
